name: HD 75426
spectral_type: F5IV/V (modern parameters indicate dwarf: Gaia logg=4.12 TIC lumclass=DWARF)
archival_rv_summary: Gaia DR3 rv_amplitude_robust = 1.92 km/s across 27 transits; 4 independent astrometric witnesses (HGCA 33-sigma + Kervella PMa 27-sigma + NSS Acceleration 60-sigma + Tycho-Gaia 25-yr DeltaPM 4.41-sigma) all consistent with single P~125yr companion
notes: orvara joint HGCA + synthetic K_RV constraint posterior median 282 MJ (early M-dwarf regime) but 2-sigma low tail 100 MJ still includes substellar; never observed by any ESO public spectrograph; single CORALIE/FEROS epoch decisive

name: HD 120954
archival_rv_summary: orvara joint HGCA + 3 RV epochs posterior; depends on prior; multi-decade ΔRV (Nordstrom 2004 to Gaia DR3) = +6.5 km/s
notes: Pipeline interpretation: P~70yr K-dwarf at edge-on inclination; 5 independent astrometric+RV signals mutually consistent; not a substellar candidate

name: HIP 91479
archival_rv_summary: Gaia DR3: 30 RV transits, rv_amplitude_robust=3.90 km/s, rv_chisq_pvalue=6.75e-5 (highly variable, peak-to-peak matches predicted K1~1.95 km/s for joint astro+spec orbit fit)
notes: AstroSpectroSB1 joint astro+spec fit. P=855.84d, e=0.815. a_phot=2.33mas -> a_1=0.127AU. From Kepler with M_1~0.7Msun: M_2=64 MJ (independent of inclination). K_1 from Gaia spec Thiele-Innes (C,H) = 574 m/s; K_1 from Gaia rv_amplitude_robust/2 = 1948 m/s — 3.4x INCONSISTENT, suggests either unmodeled second body, activity jitter, or normalization issue in Pourbaix C,H formula. Astrometric M_2 measurement is robust; spectroscopic mass interpretation more uncertain. HGCA Brandt 2024 chi^2=50.3 (independent 25-yr PM anomaly). LP 335-104 high-PM K-dwarf at 55.6 pc.
